title: USDC Prim Spec Mapping Production Contract
format_neutral_rules: contracts/document-model-productions/prim-spec.contract.json — the prim spec field set, child-list invariants, listOp subfield rules, and value-shape rules are pinned in the format-neutral production. This contract adds the USDC-specific mapping: Crate spec form 6 -> prim spec, per-field name resolution via TOKENS/FIELDS/FIELDSETS, and per-field Crate value-representation type bindings.

storage_mapping.rule: A SPECS entry with form=6 produces a prim spec at the path resolved through PATHS. Authored fields come from the FIELDSETS group whose start is given by the SPECS entry's field_set_index. Each field-id in the group resolves through FIELDS to a (token-name, value-representation-word) pair; the token name is the document-model field name, and the value-representation word is decoded by usdc-value-decoder. Child specs (other SPECS entries whose paths are descendants of this prim) are stored through usd-document-model, and the parser maintains primChildren / propertyChildren / variantSetChildren.

field_resolution.specifier: FIELDS token name 'specifier'; value type 42 (Specifier) inlined with payload encoding 0=def, 1=over, 2=class
field_resolution.typeName: FIELDS token name 'typeName'; value type 11 (token) inlined as a uint32 token index resolved through the TOKENS table
field_resolution.kind: FIELDS token name 'kind'; value type 11 (token) inlined
field_resolution.active: FIELDS token name 'active'; value type 1 (bool) inlined
field_resolution.hidden: FIELDS token name 'hidden'; value type 1 (bool) inlined
field_resolution.instanceable: FIELDS token name 'instanceable'; value type 1 (bool) inlined
field_resolution.primChildren: FIELDS token name 'primChildren'; value type 41 (TokenVector) offset-backed (uint64 count followed by uint32 token indices). When the field is absent, the parser materializes an empty token[] from child spec storage.
field_resolution.propertyChildren: FIELDS token name 'properties' (the on-disk Crate token); maps to the document-model propertyChildren field (AOUSD §7.6.2.2.2); value type 41 (TokenVector) offset-backed
field_resolution.variantSetChildren: FIELDS token name 'variantSetChildren'; value type 41 (TokenVector) offset-backed; distinct from variantSetNames
field_resolution.references: FIELDS token name 'references'; value type 35 (ReferenceListOp); each item is a native Reference record (asset path index into STRINGS, prim path index into PATHS, 16-byte LayerOffset, optional Dictionary customData)
field_resolution.payload: FIELDS token name 'payload'; value type 55 (PayloadListOp) at minor>=8 (the AOUSD 1.0.1 baseline); each item is a native Payload record (asset path index, prim path index, 16-byte LayerOffset)
field_resolution.inheritPaths: FIELDS token name 'inheritPaths'; value type 34 (PathListOp); items are uint32 path indices into PATHS
field_resolution.variantSetNames: FIELDS token name 'variantSetNames'; value type 32 (TokenListOp); distinct from variantSetChildren
field_resolution.variantSelection: FIELDS token name 'variantSelection'; value type 45 (VariantSelectionMap); uint64 count followed by alternating string-index key + string-index value entries (4-byte indices into STRINGS)
field_resolution.documentation: FIELDS token name 'documentation'; value type 10 (string) inlined as a uint32 string index into STRINGS. The 'doc' alias is a USDA grammar spelling and is not encoded in Crate; Crate stores the canonical 'documentation' token name.
field_resolution.customData: FIELDS token name 'customData'; value type 31 (dictionary) offset-backed
field_resolution.assetInfo: FIELDS token name 'assetInfo'; value type 31 (dictionary) offset-backed

storage_mapping.child_list_materialization: primChildren / propertyChildren / variantSetChildren may be authored explicitly in the Crate FIELDS table OR omitted; when omitted, the parser materializes them from the set of child specs whose paths descend from this prim. When authored, the stored value wins (the on-disk authoring is the source of truth) and child-spec storage must agree.

laws: a Crate SPECS entry with form=6 produces exactly one prim spec at the path resolved through PATHS
field names on a prim spec must come from the TOKENS table via the FIELDS table; the parser must not synthesize prim field names from substring or fixture-name patterns

alias spellings (doc, inherits, variantSets, variants) are USDA grammar concerns and do not appear in Crate FIELDS; the Crate parser binds canonical token names (documentation, inheritPaths, variantSetNames, variantSelection) directly

Reference and Payload items are stored as native arc records (asset path, prim path, LayerOffset, optional customData) consistent with document-model-productions/prim-spec.contract.json#fields.references; raw JSON dictionaries, bare strings, or string-keyed maps are forbidden in durable storage
VariantSelectionMap (value type 45) decodes to a native variantSelection map; storage must not be string-keyed JSON

the 'relocates' token is an AOUSD §7.6.2.7 deprecated prim field and, under strict spec conformance, is rejected with MalformedUsdcSection on a form-6 spec; layer relocates are decoded only as the canonical layerRelocates field on the form-7 layer spec (value type 58), never on prims
golden_groups: Crate form 6 specs decode to prim specs at the expected paths
specifier / typeName / kind / active / hidden / instanceable round-trip on prim specs
primChildren materialized from child spec storage when absent
primChildren preserved exactly when authored explicitly in Crate
references / payload with asset, prim path, and LayerOffset
inheritPaths / specializes single-item and multi-item listOps with prepend/append/delete subfields
variantSetNames (listOp<token>) vs variantSetChildren (spec storage) maintained independently
variantSelection (VariantSelectionMap value type 45) decoded as native map
